title: ANCE00178S101 What is a Coronary Angioplasty
text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open.

title: ANCE00178S101 What is a Coronary Angioplasty
text_en: Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon. The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire.

title: ANCE00178S101 What is a Coronary Angioplasty
text_en: When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place. After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery open.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your groin.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: A needle will be placed through your skin and into the femoral artery.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery. Then, the needle will be withdrawn. It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access into your artery.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart. A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: The progress of the procedure will be checked with an x-ray device called a flora scope.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: At this point, your doctor will remove the guide wire.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: Then, the doctor will move the tip of the catheter just inside the coronary artery to be examined.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
text_en: A special die will be injected into the artery. This allows your doctor to view it better with the flora scope. The die will make any blockages in the artery stand out.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
text_en: If a significant blockage is found, your doctor will insert a guide wire into the artery.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
text_en: A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be moved along the wire to the blockage. When the balloon inflates, it will expand the artery and improve the blood flow. You may feel some chest discomfort while this is happening.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
text_en: After this, your doctor will deflate and remove the balloon.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open. Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
text_en: The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire. When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
text_en: After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery open.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Coronary artery angiography is an x-ray test of the heart. This test can view blood vessels in your heart called coronary arteries. It can find out if a waxy substance called plaque has narrowed these vessels. And, it can show how well the valves and chambers of your heart are working. Coronary angiography is often done with a medical procedure called cardiac catheterization.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Before the procedure, you will be placed on a table with an x-ray device called a florescope.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Usually cardiac catheterization takes place through the radial artery in your wrist. This route normally has less bleeding and complications. Sometimes the femoral artery in your groin may be used instead.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: To begin, the doctor will numb the skin in your wrist. A needle will be inserted into your radial artery.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: A wire will be passed through this needle. The wire will be gently guided through your arteries to your heart.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: The needle will be taken out and replaced with a small flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access to your radial artery.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Next, a soft flexible tube called a catheter will be slipped over the wire through the sheath. The catheter will be threaded up to your heart.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Then, the florescope will be moved around your chest to take x-ray images of your heart. Your doctor will use the images to check on the catheter. You may feel pressure as the wire and sheath are put in, but you will not feel them inside your arteries.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Once the catheter reaches your coronary arteries, the wire will be removed. The catheter tip will be placed just inside each artery to inject a special die. This die allows the florescope to take x-ray pictures of your arteries, called angiograms.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: Any blockages will be clearly identified as the arteries fill with die. You may feel flushed or slightly nauseated when the die is injected.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: At the end of the procedure, the catheter will be removed through your wrist. A pressure band will be used over the radial artery to prevent bleeding. Then, the sheath will be removed. The pressure band will remain on your wrist until bleeding has stopped.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
text_en: To find out more about coronary angiography, talk to your healthcare provider.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
text_en: If you or someone you know has recently had a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage, this video will help you understand the steps of the procedure and how it restores blood flow to your heart.
